name: Finish UI + theme editor

goal.answer: Finish the remaining work of the paused "Match open-carrusel UI + theme editor" ferment under a fresh ID: verify the dashboard + editor layouts match open-carrusel's screenshots (Phase 1 steps 1-2 already done — globals.css reverted to exact light/coral tokens, dark-color audit clean), then build the Open Design-style visual theme editor with custom-theme CRUD so users can edit palette/fonts/spacing live and persist custom themes alongside the 15 presets.

criteria.answer:
App chrome tokens still match open-carrusel exactly (regression baseline — steps 1-2 already done): globals.css --background:#fafafa, --surface:#ffffff, --accent:#e94560, --border:#e5e5e5 — verified by grep of globals.css
Dashboard layout matches open-carrusel screenshot: header + 'My Carousels'/'Templates' tabs + 3-col card grid (thumbnail, name, aspect-ratio badge, date, hover duplicate/delete) — verified by manual visual check
Editor layout matches open-carrusel screenshot: 3-panel (chat left w-80 / preview center / filmstrip bottom) + top toolbar with aspect selector + actions — verified by manual visual check
Visual theme editor opens from the editor: 5 color pickers (primary/secondary/accent/background/surface), heading+body font dropdowns, spacing scale; preview iframe updates live on change — verified by editing a field and observing instant preview update
Custom themes persist: 'Save as custom theme' writes to /data/custom-themes.json and survives reload — verified by saving, reloading, and re-checking the theme gallery
Custom themes appear in ThemeGallery alongside the 15 presets, with edit + delete controls — verified by saving a theme and confirming it shows in the gallery with controls
npm run build exits 0 with zero TypeScript errors — verified by build command
npm run doctor passes all required checks — verified by doctor command

constraints.answer: UI tokens must match open-carrusel's globals.css exactly (#fafafa bg / #ffffff surface / #e94560 coral accent / #e5e5e5 border) — already done in step 1, keep as regression baseline, Preserve all existing functionality: 15 preset themes, dual-mode LLM adapter, watermark, license, PDF+PNG export — no regressions, Custom themes MUST reuse the existing Theme type (src/types/theme.ts) so presets and custom themes render identically via the existing parser/serializer, Reuse the existing ColorPicker (src/components/brand/ColorPicker.tsx) and FontSelector (src/components/brand/FontSelector.tsx) components — do NOT build new pickers or add new dependencies, Custom-theme storage MUST mirror the style-presets.ts pattern exactly (readDataSafe/writeData + generateId/now over a JSON file in /data), Theme editor is the ONLY Open Design-style feature in scope; design-system import, per-slide sliders, and clone-from-carousel remain out of scope (roadmap), internaldev.md remains the source of truth for overall project direction

phases.answer: UI alignment verification, Visual theme editor + custom-theme CRUD

assumptions.answer:
This ferment re-scopes the remaining work of the paused UI ferment (019f1078) under a fresh ID, per the user's explicit choice to "finish the paused UI ferment's work." Steps 1-2 of the original Phase 1 are already complete and verified: globals.css was reverted to open-carrusel's exact light/coral tokens (#fafafa/#ffffff/#e94560/#e5e5e5), and the dark-color audit found only one token-bypass (bg-[#f0f0f0] in CarouselPreview.tsx) which was fixed to bg-muted. The Midnight Neon dark theme remains as one of the 15 carousel-content presets; the app chrome itself is light.

Exploration confirmed every reuse assumption: (1) ColorPicker.tsx exists with props {label, value, onChange} and is reused for all 5 color pickers; (2) FontSelector.tsx exists with the same prop shape and fetches its font list from /api/fonts, reused for heading+body; (3) style-presets.ts provides the exact CRUD pattern to mirror (readDataSafe/writeData from ./data, generateId/now from ./utils, FILE constant, load/save helpers, list/get/create/delete functions); (4) data.ts exports readDataSafe + writeData with atomic writes + async-mutex already in place; (5) the Theme type in src/types/theme.ts is the full contract (id/name/category/atmosphere/palette/fonts/spacing/motion/designRules) that custom themes conform to. Custom themes are stored as JSON in /data/custom-themes.json (editable) and normalized to the Theme type so preview/export treat them identically to presets.

The visual theme editor edits the carousel's active theme instance live (a working copy) and 'Save as custom theme' persists it. The 15 preset DESIGN.md files are read-only. Design-system import, per-slide adjustment sliders, and clone-from-carousel are explicitly out of scope (roadmap). "Proceed to next phase" / deployment / Lemon Squeezy product setup are separate future work, not part of this ferment.

phases.name: UI alignment verification
phases.goal: Finish the remaining verification of the original Phase 1 (steps 3-5): confirm the dashboard and editor layouts match open-carrusel's screenshots and pass the build+doctor gate. Steps 1-2 (token revert + dark-color audit) are already done.
phases.description: Light, confirmatory phase. The dashboard and editor layouts were inherited from open-carrusel during scaffolding, so this phase verifies no regressions from the Phase E rebrand and confirms the tokens revert (step 1) propagated correctly. Phase 2's theme editor opens within this verified editor page, so the layout must be confirmed intact first.
constraints: Do not regress the 15 themes, LLM adapter, watermark, license, or export
Match open-carrusel layout structure (3-panel editor, dashboard tabs)

steps.description: Verify dashboard (src/app/page.tsx) matches open-carrusel screenshot: header with 'Slidr' title + 'New Carousel' button, 'My Carousels'/'Templates' tabs, 3-col card grid with thumbnail/aspect-badge/date/hover-actions. Read the file and confirm all elements present (inherited from open-carrusel — confirm no Phase E rebrand regressions).

verification.command: grep -q 'My Carousels' src/app/page.tsx && grep -q 'Templates' src/app/page.tsx && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Verify editor (src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx) matches open-carrusel screenshot: 3-panel (chat left w-80 / preview center / filmstrip bottom) + top toolbar with aspect selector. Confirm the Phase B ThemeGallery/SizeSelector/LlmConfigModal additions don't break the inherited 3-panel layout.

verification.command: grep -q 'border-r border-border' src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx && grep -q 'SlideFilmstrip' src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Run npm run build (zero TS errors) and npm run doctor (all required checks pass) as the gate for this phase. Confirms the token revert + CarouselPreview bg-muted fix compile cleanly.

verification.command: npm run build > /tmp/slidr-build.log 2>&1 && ! grep -qiE 'error TS|cannot find|cannot be' /tmp/slidr-build.log && echo BUILD_PASS || (tail -20 /tmp/slidr-build.log; echo BUILD_FAIL)

phases.name: Visual theme editor + custom-theme CRUD
phases.goal: Add an Open Design-style visual theme editor: live palette/font/spacing editing on the carousel's active theme, with 'Save as custom theme' persisting to /data/custom-themes.json and custom themes appearing in the ThemeGallery with edit/delete controls.
phases.description: Builds on Phase 1's verified editor page. Adds the customization layer. Custom themes are JSON (editable) normalized to the existing Theme type so preview/export treat them identically to presets. Reuses the existing ColorPicker/FontSelector brand components — no new dependencies.
constraints: Mirror the style-presets.ts CRUD pattern exactly for custom-store.ts
Reuse ColorPicker + FontSelector from src/components/brand/ — no new pickers, no new deps
Custom themes conform to the Theme type in src/types/theme.ts
Design-system import, per-slide sliders, clone-from-carousel are out of scope (roadmap)

steps.description: Create src/lib/themes/custom-store.ts mirroring src/lib/style-presets.ts exactly: import readDataSafe/writeData from ../data, generateId/now from ../utils; FILE = 'custom-themes.json'; load()/save() helpers; export listCustomThemes(): Promise<Theme[]>, getCustomTheme(id): Promise<Theme|null>, saveCustomTheme(params: Omit<Theme,'id'> & {id?:string}): Promise<Theme> (create with generateId+now if no id, else update in place), deleteCustomTheme(id): Promise<boolean>. Update src/lib/themes/index.ts to re-export these plus a new listAllThemes(): Promise<Theme[]> that merges listThemes() (presets) + listCustomThemes().

verification.command: test -f src/lib/themes/custom-store.ts && grep -q 'export async function listCustomThemes' src/lib/themes/custom-store.ts && grep -q 'export async function saveCustomTheme' src/lib/themes/custom-store.ts && grep -q 'listAllThemes' src/lib/themes/index.ts && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Add API routes: src/app/api/themes/custom/route.ts (GET → listCustomThemes, POST → saveCustomTheme with body) and src/app/api/themes/custom/[id]/route.ts (PUT → saveCustomTheme with id, DELETE → deleteCustomTheme). Update GET /api/themes (src/app/api/themes/route.ts) to return listAllThemes() (merged presets+custom) so the gallery fetches one list.

verification.command: test -f src/app/api/themes/custom/route.ts && test -f 'src/app/api/themes/custom/[id]/route.ts' && grep -q 'listAllThemes\|listCustomThemes' src/app/api/themes/route.ts && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Create src/components/themes/ThemeEditor.tsx modal: 5 ColorPicker instances (reuse src/components/brand/ColorPicker.tsx) for palette.primary/secondary/accent/background/surface; 2 FontSelector instances (reuse src/components/brand/FontSelector.tsx) for fonts.heading/body; a spacing scale input (number[] editor for spacing.scale) + padding/gap text inputs. Props: { theme: Theme; onChange: (t: Theme) => void; onSave: () => void; onClose: () => void; onDelete?: () => void; }. Live preview is driven by the parent passing onChange updates up to the carousel's working-copy theme.

verification.command: test -f src/components/themes/ThemeEditor.tsx && grep -q 'ColorPicker' src/components/themes/ThemeEditor.tsx && grep -q 'FontSelector' src/components/themes/ThemeEditor.tsx && grep -c 'ColorPicker' src/components/themes/ThemeEditor.tsx | grep -q '[5-9]' && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Update src/components/themes/ThemeGallery.tsx: fetch merged presets+custom via GET /api/themes; render custom themes in a separate 'Custom' section with Edit (opens ThemeEditor) + Delete buttons; add an 'Edit theme' button on the currently-selected theme and a 'New custom theme' button at top that opens ThemeEditor with a blank theme.

verification.command: grep -qi 'custom' src/components/themes/ThemeGallery.tsx && grep -q 'ThemeEditor' src/components/themes/ThemeGallery.tsx && grep -qi 'New custom theme' src/components/themes/ThemeGallery.tsx && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Wire ThemeEditor into the editor page (src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx): add state showThemeEditor + editingTheme (working-copy Theme). When user edits via the editor, update editingTheme (overrides the carousel.themeId-bound theme for the live preview passed to CarouselPreview/ChatPanel). On 'Save as custom theme', POST to /api/themes/custom, then PUT the carousel with the new custom themeId via /api/carousels/[id]. On Edit existing custom theme, PUT the updated theme.

verification.command: grep -q 'ThemeEditor' src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx && grep -q 'showThemeEditor' src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx && grep -q 'editingTheme' src/app/carousel/[id]/page.tsx && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Update src/lib/chat-system-prompt.ts to accept a resolved Theme object (preset OR custom) instead of only looking up by themeId — so custom themes inject their palette/fonts/designRules into the LLM prompt identically to presets. Add a theme param (or a resolved-theme lookup that checks presets then custom-store) to the prompt builder.

verification.command: grep -q 'Theme' src/lib/chat-system-prompt.ts && grep -qiE 'palette|fonts|designRules' src/lib/chat-system-prompt.ts && echo PASS || echo FAIL

steps.description: Run npm run build (zero TS errors) + npm run doctor (all required checks pass) + manual verification: open editor, pick a theme, open ThemeEditor, change accent color, confirm preview iframe updates live, save as custom theme, reload page, confirm custom theme persists in the gallery with edit/delete controls.

verification.command: npm run build > /tmp/slidr-build2.log 2>&1 && ! grep -qiE 'error TS|cannot find|cannot be' /tmp/slidr-build2.log && echo BUILD_PASS || (tail -20 /tmp/slidr-build2.log; echo BUILD_FAIL)

goal: Finish the remaining work of the paused "Match open-carrusel UI + theme editor" ferment under a fresh ID: verify the dashboard + editor layouts match open-carrusel's screenshots (Phase 1 steps 1-2 already done — globals.css reverted to exact light/coral tokens, dark-color audit clean), then build the Open Design-style visual theme editor with custom-theme CRUD so users can edit palette/fonts/spacing live and persist custom themes alongside the 15 presets.
successCriteria: App chrome tokens still match open-carrusel exactly (regression baseline — steps 1-2 already done): globals.css --background:#fafafa, --surface:#ffffff, --accent:#e94560, --border:#e5e5e5 — verified by grep of globals.css

Dashboard layout matches open-carrusel screenshot: header + 'My Carousels'/'Templates' tabs + 3-col card grid (thumbnail, name, aspect-ratio badge, date, hover duplicate/delete) — verified by manual visual check
Editor layout matches open-carrusel screenshot: 3-panel (chat left w-80 / preview center / filmstrip bottom) + top toolbar with aspect selector + actions — verified by manual visual check

Visual theme editor opens from the editor: 5 color pickers (primary/secondary/accent/background/surface), heading+body font dropdowns, spacing scale; preview iframe updates live on change — verified by editing a field and observing instant preview update
Custom themes persist: 'Save as custom theme' writes to /data/custom-themes.json and survives reload — verified by saving, reloading, and re-checking the theme gallery
Custom themes appear in ThemeGallery alongside the 15 presets, with edit + delete controls — verified by saving a theme and confirming it shows in the gallery with controls
npm run build exits 0 with zero TypeScript errors — verified by build command
npm run doctor passes all required checks — verified by doctor command
constraints: UI tokens must match open-carrusel's globals.css exactly (#fafafa bg / #ffffff surface / #e94560 coral accent / #e5e5e5 border) — already done in step 1, keep as regression baseline
Preserve all existing functionality: 15 preset themes, dual-mode LLM adapter, watermark, license, PDF+PNG export — no regressions
Custom themes MUST reuse the existing Theme type (src/types/theme.ts) so presets and custom themes render identically via the existing parser/serializer
Reuse the existing ColorPicker (src/components/brand/ColorPicker.tsx) and FontSelector (src/components/brand/FontSelector.tsx) components — do NOT build new pickers or add new dependencies
Custom-theme storage MUST mirror the style-presets.ts pattern exactly (readDataSafe/writeData + generateId/now over a JSON file in /data)
Theme editor is the ONLY Open Design-style feature in scope; design-system import, per-slide sliders, and clone-from-carousel remain out of scope (roadmap)
internaldev.md remains the source of truth for overall project direction
